text: This video presents the modified robotic-assisted nephro-ureterectomy and partial cystectomy for a UVJ tumor. The surgery was performed by Dr. Huang Zhaoyuan at Department of Urology, National Taiwan University Hospital, Taiwan.
The fifty-two-year-old man has a past history of hypertension. He presented painless gross hematuria and the CT scan showed a left UVJ tumor with hydronephrosis.

The patient is positioned in the full flank position with the affected side facing up. The surgical team adjusts the robotic system for ureterectomy and partial cystectomy following kidney dissection. The ports are rearranged: the first robotic arm's port is now an assistant port, the second robotic arm's port is converted to the first robotic arm's port, and the assistant port is changed to the second robotic arm's port.
The surgeon dissects the descending colon from the surface of Gerota's fascia, exposing the left ureter, which is then identified and looped.
The surgeon opens the rotous fascia, revealing the renal artery through careful dissection. The renal artery is then ligated.
The surgeon uses an endo-GIA stapler to ligate the renal vein, accommodating its wide diameter.

The surgeon manipulates the ureterovesical junction (UVJ) after separating the kidney and upper ureter from surrounding tissue. A stay suture is placed for further dissection. Partial cystectomy is performed by dissecting the detrusor muscle, revealing the urothelial mucosa with a tenting pattern.
The surgeon repairs the bladder using a continuous v-lock suture, securing the tissue edges together.
A seven-centimeter incision is made over the lower abdominal midline near the umbilicus for the extraction procedure, with visible blood loss amounting to approximately 150 milliliters.
